content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
, File 
Date 
'December 6, 1985 
No. M-85-251 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Kathy Donovan 
Subject: Telephone Advice to Chris Zirkle, Legislative Counsel's Office, 
Regarding Legislative Newsletters 
Chris Zirkle, of the Legislative Counsel's Office, requested 
telephone advice concerning Government Code section 89001, which 
prohibits an elected state officer from sending any newsletter or 
other mass mailing at public expense after the elected state 
officer has filed a declaration of candidacy for any office. 
In 
accordance with regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code section 18901, 
information or materials sent in response to a specific request 
contained in written correspondence, petitions, oral inquiries, or 
coupons, are not considered a mass mailing subject to the 
restrictions of section 89001. 
Mr. Zirkle asked whether a 
legislator who sends a newsletter which includes a coupon for 
constituents to use in requesting specific information or 
materials, may use the newsletter to inform the constituent that 
the Political Reform Act prohibits the legislator from sending 
additional newsletters unless the constituent completes and 
returns the coupon. 
I advised Mr. Zirkle that we think section 89001 permits a 
legislator to send a newsletter with that type of message, 
although I emphasized that the newsletter should provide an 
accurate explanation of section 89001. 
Mr. Zirkle requested that 
I provide him with an example of an explanation which we would 
consider sufficient. 
I suggested the following: 
Enclosed is a coupon which you may use to request 
information or materials on specific legislative matters from 
this office. 
After an incumbent legislator has filed a 
declaration of candidacy for any office, the Political Reform 
Act prohibits the legislator from sending newsletters or other 
materials at public expense unless the information or 
materials are sent in response to specific requests. 
If you 
complete and return the enclosed coupon, we will continue to 
send you information about the legislative matters which 
interest you.

sections.question: Whether a legislator who sends a newsletter which includes a coupon for constituents to use in requesting specific information or materials, may use the newsletter to inform the constituent that the Political Reform Act prohibits the legislator from sending additional newsletters unless the constituent completes and returns the coupon.
sections.conclusion: I advised Mr. Zirkle that we think section 89001 permits a legislator to send a newsletter with that type of message, although I emphasized that the newsletter should provide an accurate explanation of section 89001.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a legislator include in a newsletter a message explaining that after filing a declaration of candidacy, they are prohibited from sending newsletters at public expense unless constituents submit a coupon requesting specific information?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advises that Government Code section 89001 permits a legislator to include such a message in a newsletter, provided the newsletter includes an accurate explanation of the restriction. The FPPC provided a sample explanation of the restriction for use in the newsletter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is formatted as a memorandum rather than traditional advice letter format. Question and conclusion sections are embedded in narrative text. Document ID M-85-251 corresponds to year 1985. FPPC provided sample language for the legislator to use in communicating the restriction to constituents.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a legislator who sends a newsletter which includes a coupon for constituents to use in requesting specific information or materials, may use the newsletter to inform the constituent that the Political Reform Act prohibits the legislator from sending additional newsletters unless the constituent completes and returns the coupon.

CONCLUSION: I advised Mr. Zirkle that we think section 89001 permits a legislator to send a newsletter with that type of message, although I emphasized that the newsletter should provide an accurate explanation of section 89001.

In accordance with regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code section 18901, information or materials sent in response to a specific request contained in written correspondence, petitions, oral inquiries, or coupons, are not considered a mass mailing subject to the restrictions of section 89001. Mr. Zirkle asked whether a legislator who sends a newsletter which includes a coupon for constituents to use in requesting specific information or materials, may use the newsletter to inform the constituent that the Political Reform Act prohibits the legislator from sending additional newsletters unless the constituent completes and returns the coupon. I advised Mr. Zirkle that we think section 89001 permits a legislator to send a newsletter with that type of message, although I emphasized that the newsletter should provide an accurate explanation of section 89001. embedding.summary: FPPC provides telephone advice to the Legislative Counsel's Office regarding Government Code section 89001, which restricts newsletters sent by candidates for office. The FPPC advises that legislators may include a message in newsletters explaining this restriction and requesting constituents return a coupon to receive further communications.
